title: Dense Floral Peruvian Paprika Braise
description: A hearty, flavorful, and aromatic braise originating from Peru, featuring dense and floral notes from paprika and other spices, perfect for a comforting low-carb meal after a monsoon.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika for a rich, earthy flavor

ingredients.name: Aji amarillo peppers
ingredients.notes: Peruvian yellow peppers for a tangy and slightly sweet flavor

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: For depth and a touch of sweetness

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: For a savory and aromatic flavor

ingredients.name: Ground beef
ingredients.notes: Choose a lean cut for a low-carb-friendly option

ingredients.name: Beef broth
ingredients.notes: Homemade or low-sodium store-bought for a rich flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Concentrated tomato paste for a deep and intense flavor

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground cumin for a warm and earthy flavor

ingredients.name: Cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: For a touch of heat and spice

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: To enhance flavors and balance seasoning

ingredients.name: Fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: For a fresh and herbal garnish

instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add more oil if necessary, then sauté the sliced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the sliced Aji amarillo peppers and cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the beef broth and tomato paste. Stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the browned beef to the pot, cover it, and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the beef in the preheated oven at 300°F (150°C) for 2 hours, or until it reaches your desired level of tenderness.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
